title: Hearing impairment geriatric review

treatment_and_management.text: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact adult hearing section supports interval hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, audiometry, tympanometry, personalised discussion and reviewed outcomes without recommending a device. The exact assessment section supports clinician-entered plan, follow-up and questions based on reviewed hearing needs and results.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

source.title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.8.1 — audiology assessment, management, and support

referral.text: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.
referral.rationale: The exact section supports clinician-performed assessment and concern documentation without generating referral.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

source_limitations: Cause, exact audiology results, cognition, device status, diagnosis, treatment, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
